message:
🐛 Show degraded state when agent connected but cluster data missing (#10510) (#10515)

When kagenti shows connected but cluster data is unavailable, display an
informative empty state instead of the generic 'No clusters found' message.
Distinguishes between agent-connected-no-data and cluster-mode-limited-scope
scenarios with appropriate messaging.

Signed-off-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <copilot@github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -3181,6 +3181,10 @@
     "importImporting": "Importing...",
     "noClusterTitle": "No clusters found",
     "noClusterDesc": "Add a cluster to your kubeconfig to get started.",
+    "agentConnectedNoDataTitle": "Agent connected — no cluster data available",
+    "agentConnectedNoDataDesc": "The agent is connected but no cluster data was returned. The agent may still be initializing, or the connected clusters may not be reporting inventory yet.",
+    "clusterModeNoDataTitle": "Running in cluster mode — no data available",
+    "clusterModeNoDataDesc": "The console is running in cluster mode but no cluster data is available. This may happen when the service account has limited RBAC scope.",
     "connectStep1": "Server",
     "connectStep2": "Authentication",
     "connectStep3": "Context",
